root_cause: StatefulSet rollout cannot start because updateStrategy partition excludes every replica
evidence: StatefulSet 'ledger-db' has replicas=3 but RollingUpdate partition=3, so no existing ordinal qualifies for update
StatefulSet 'ledger-db' observed generation 7/7 with currentRevision 'ledger-db-6b6b6d4c9f' and updateRevision 'ledger-db-7d889b5d7c'
updatedReplicas remains 0 while currentReplicas=3 and readyReplicas=3

Observed 2 recent partition-related controller event(s) over 12.0 minutes; latest: StatefulSet ledger-db still reports currentRevision ledger-db-6b6b6d4c9f and updateRevision ledger-db-7d889b5d7c with partition 3; 0 Pods updated
Pod 'ledger-db-1' remains on current revision 'ledger-db-6b6b6d4c9f' because ordinal 1 is below partition 3

causes.message: StatefulSet 'ledger-db' has observed a new revision 'ledger-db-7d889b5d7c' while existing Pods still run revision 'ledger-db-6b6b6d4c9f'
RollingUpdate partition 3 is above the highest existing ordinal 2, so no Pod is eligible for update
The StatefulSet controller cannot begin the rolling update because zero ordinals satisfy the configured partition
All current replicas remain on the old revision and updatedReplicas is still 0

pod:ledger-db-1: Ordinal 1 is below partition 3, so this Pod stays on revision 'ledger-db-6b6b6d4c9f'

likely_causes: A canary or staged RollingUpdate partition was left at the replica count after an earlier maintenance step
The partition value was copied from a larger StatefulSet or from a previous scale and now exceeds the highest live ordinal
Automation patched spec.updateStrategy.rollingUpdate.partition to pause rollout but never lowered it to resume updates
A manual StatefulSet edit introduced a new template revision while leaving the rollout partition set too high
suggested_checks: Compare spec.updateStrategy.rollingUpdate.partition with the highest current ordinal and desired replica count
Lower or remove the partition after confirming which ordinals should actually receive the new revision
